zh.lifeSummary: 當George Lewis於1557年出生在英國肯特布藍利時，他的父親Hugh Lewis37歲，母親Gwladys Ferch Ieuan37歲。他於1574年10月23日與Mary Foster在英國肯特坎特布瑞結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1631年7月11日在他的家鄉享年74歲。

nameMeanings.name: Lewis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Lewis, Leweis, Lowis, from ancient Germanic (originally West Frankish) Hludwig (itself from hlōd- ‘fame, famous’ + wīg- ‘battle’). This was Latinized as Ludovicus and Chlodovisus, which were gallicized as Clovis or Clouis, French Louis. The name may also appear as Lawis, Laweys, Lawes, by unrounding of the vowel of Lowis on the analogy of the variation between Low and Law as pet forms of Middle English Lourence alias Laurence. This surname is also very common among African Americans. See Laws 2 and compare Lawrence .
Welsh: adopted for the Welsh personal name Llywelyn (see Llewellyn ).

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Lughaidh ‘son of Lughaidh’. This is one of the most common Old Irish personal names. It is derived from Lugh ‘brightness’, which was the name of a Celtic god.

variants.name: Laws
meanings: English: variant of Law (pet form of Lawrence ), with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Perhaps a shortened form of Scottish or Irish   McLeish  . Compare   McLaws  .

variants.name: Lewison
meanings: Welsh and English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name   Lewis   (in Wales this was taken as equivalent to the Welsh personal name   Llewelyn  ). In North America, this surname has reportedly absorbed in some cases the Greek patronymic   Iliopoulos  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): patronymic from the personal name Lewi, Polish-influenced form of Levy .

variants.name: McGlew
meanings: Irish: most probably from Mac Lughaidh ‘son of Lughaidh’, a Gaelic personal name (referencing the god Lugh), sometimes Anglicized as Lewis . However, both Woulfe and MacLysaght consider McGlew to be a shortened form of Mac Dhuinnshléibhe (see McAleavey and Dunleavy ).

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When George Lewis was born in 1557, in Brenchley, Kent, England, his father, Hugh Lewis, was 37 and his mother, Gwladys Ferch Ieuan, was 37. He married Mary Foster on 23 October 1574, in Canterbury, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 11 July 1631, in his hometown, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque George Lewis est né en 1557, à Brenchley, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Hugh Lewis, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Gwladys Ferch Ieuan, avait 37 ans. Il a épousé Mary Foster le 23 octobre 1574, à Canterbury, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 11 juillet 1631, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 74 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als George Lewis 1557 in Brenchley, Kent, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hugh Lewis 37 und seine Mutter Gwladys Ferch Ieuan 37. Er hat am 23. Oktober 1574 in Canterbury, Kent, England Mary Foster geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 11. Juli 1631 im Alter von 74 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando George Lewis è nato il nel 1557 nel Brenchley, Kent, England, il suo padre, Hugh Lewis, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Gwladys Ferch Ieuan, aveva 37 anni. Lui sposato Mary Foster il 23 ottobre 1574, nel Canterbury, Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 11 luglio 1631, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 74 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: George Lewis(이)가 1557년 영국 켄트 브렌클리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh Lewis(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Gwladys Ferch Ieuan(은)는 37세였다. 그는 1574년 10월 23일 영국 켄트 캉르버이에서 Mary Foster(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1631년 7월 11일 그의 고향에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando George Lewis nasceu em 1557, em Brenchley, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Hugh Lewis, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Gwladys Ferch Ieuan, tinha 37 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Foster em 23 de outubro de 1574, em Canterbury, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 11 de julho de 1631, em sua cidade natal, com 74 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда George Lewis родился в 1557 г. в местности бренчли, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени Hugh Lewis было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Gwladys Ferch Ieuan, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Foster 23 октября 1574 г. в местности кантирбури, кэнт, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 11 июля 1631 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 74 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando George Lewis nació en 1557, en Brenchley, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Hugh Lewis, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Gwladys Ferch Ieuan, tenía 37 años. Se casó con Mary Foster el 23 de octubre de 1574, en Canterbury, Kent, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 11 de julio de 1631, en su pueblo, a la edad de 74 años.
